title: API version '2023-10': date field issue
excerpt:
Hi, 
  
In the old API, the ‘text’ of the Date field was returned based on the timezone of the user profile: 
  
However, in the new API (‘2023-10’), the ‘text’ is returned in UTC: 
  
This is causing our calendar app to…

posts.author: yarhuoy (Ng Yar Huoy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

01 Kuala Lumpur Timezone (GMT+08:00)1331×249 26.4 KB

In the old API, the ‘text’ of the Date field was returned based on the timezone of the user profile:

02 Old version API1339×580 132 KB

However, in the new API (‘2023-10’), the ‘text’ is returned in UTC:

03 New version API1296×374 86.7 KB

This is causing our calendar app to display all date times incorrectly for users. Is there a way to obtain the ‘converted’ timezone date value without specifically retrieving the timezone from the user’s profile and performing additional logic on our own?

Br, YH

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @yarhuoy

That is a very painfull (and undocumented) change indeed. Did you contact the appsupport team on this one? If so, curious to know their reply. @Matias.Monday

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @yarhuoy and @basdebruin – let me look into this and get back to you about it. Will update here with my findings.

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey team, this is indeed an issue - was able to reproduce it pretty easily in my demo account. Will pass it over to our R&D team to get it fixed!

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Internal - link to DOW

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @dipro

Can you confirm this will be fixed before API 2023-10 gets promoted to stable? In other words: is this considered a bug or not?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, correct

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: This was fixed! FYI @yarhuoy @basdebruin

posts.author: yarhuoy (Ng Yar Huoy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dipro ,

I’ve confirmed that this issue has indeed been fixed! Thank you for your hard work!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the kind words @yarhuoy !
